text:
Market today: Rotation is the story - money moving out of momentum tech into other sectors. Headlines flagging Sandisk/Micron sinking and CoreWeave/Nebius tumbling tell you the chip/AI-cloud complex is under pressure today. That hits your book directly.

Your holdings: SOXL (-14.52%) is the one screaming for attention - it's a 3X leveraged chip ETF with decay built in, and it's already deep in the red with your stop at $200 (price $217.55, not far above). SPAL (-13.72%) is a brand-new, very volatile leveraged IPO also bleeding fast - stop at $21.38 with price at $21.70, razor-thin cushion. SMH (-2.5%) is leaking too, consistent with today's chip rotation. On margin, these three compound the pain fast.

Watch: Whether SMH holds above $600 as a tell for broader chip sentiment. Also watch if the rotation deepens into close - leveraged positions get ugly fast.

Bottom line: Protect SOXL and SPAL first - both stops are dangerously close and margin makes every dollar of drop cost more.
plays: SOXL: Stop at $200 is your line - consider tightening it toward $210 given the -14.5% hole and 3X decay; don't let a bad trade become a disaster on margin.
SPAL: Stop at $21.38 is basically right here at $21.70 - know your exit and be ready to act; no reason to add into a leveraged name bleeding on a rotation day.
No new buys today - chip rotation and leveraged exposure already on the table; hold cash dry and let the dust settle.
